Class PlayerDeathEvent

java.lang.Object
org.bukkit.event.Event
org.bukkit.event.entity.EntityEvent
org.bukkit.event.entity.EntityDeathEvent
org.bukkit.event.entity.PlayerDeathEvent
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cancellable
public class PlayerDeathEvent extends EntityDeathEvent
Thrown whenever a Player dies

    A mutable collection to add items that the player should retain in their inventory on death (Similar to KeepInventory game rule)

    • getShowDeathMessages

      public boolean getShowDeathMessages()
      Get whether the death message should be shown. By default, this is determined by GameRules.SHOW_DEATH_MESSAGES.
      Returns:
      whether the death message should be shown
      See Also:
      • deathMessage()
      • deathScreenMessageOverride()

      public void setShowDeathMessages(boolean displayDeathMessage)

      displayDeathMessage - whether the death message should be shown

    • getPlayer

      @NotNull public @NotNull Player getPlayer()
      Clarity method for getting the player. Not really needed except for reasons of clarity.
      Returns:
      Player who is involved in this event

    • getNewExp

      public int getNewExp()
      Gets how much EXP the Player should have at respawn.

      This does not indicate how much EXP should be dropped, please see EntityDeathEvent.getDroppedExp() for that.

      Returns:
      New EXP of the respawned player

    • setNewExp

      public void setNewExp(int exp)
      Sets how much EXP the Player should have at respawn.

      This does not indicate how much EXP should be dropped, please see EntityDeathEvent.setDroppedExp(int) for that.

      Parameters:
      exp - New EXP of the respawned player

      @Nullable @Deprecated public @Nullable String getDeathMessage()

      Get the death message that will appear to everyone on the server.

    • deathScreenMessageOverride

      public void deathScreenMessageOverride(@Nullable @Nullable Component deathScreenMessageOverride)
      Overrides the death message that will appear on the death screen of the dying player. By default, this is null.

      If set to null, death screen message will be same as deathMessage().

      If the message exceeds 256 characters it will be truncated.

      Parameters:
      deathScreenMessageOverride - Message to appear on the death screen to the dying player.

    • deathScreenMessageOverride

      @Nullable public @Nullable Component deathScreenMessageOverride()
      Get the death message override that will appear on the death screen of the dying player. By default, this is null.

      If set to null, death screen message will be same as deathMessage().

      Returns:
      Message to appear on the death screen to the dying player.

    • shouldDropExperience

      public boolean shouldDropExperience()
      Returns:
      should experience be dropped from this death

    • setShouldDropExperience

      public void setShouldDropExperience(boolean doExpDrop)
      Parameters:
      doExpDrop - sets if experience should be dropped from this death

    • getKeepLevel

      public boolean getKeepLevel()
      Gets if the Player should keep all EXP at respawn.

      This flag overrides other EXP settings

      Returns:
      true if Player should keep all pre-death exp

    • setKeepLevel

      public void setKeepLevel(boolean keepLevel)
      Sets if the Player should keep all EXP at respawn.

      This overrides all other EXP settings

      This doesn't prevent the EXP from dropping. EntityDeathEvent.setDroppedExp(int) should be used stop the EXP from dropping.

      Parameters:
      keepLevel - true to keep all current value levels

    • setKeepInventory

      public void setKeepInventory(boolean keepInventory)
      Sets if the Player keeps inventory on death.

      This doesn't prevent the items from dropping. getDrops().clear() should be used stop the items from dropping.

      Parameters:
      keepInventory - true to keep the inventory

    • getKeepInventory

      public boolean getKeepInventory()
      Gets if the Player keeps inventory on death.
      Returns:
      true if the player keeps inventory on death

    • getItemsToKeep

      @NotNull public @NotNull List<ItemStack> getItemsToKeep()
      A mutable collection to add items that the player should retain in their inventory on death (Similar to KeepInventory game rule)
      You MUST remove the item from the .getDrops() collection too or it will duplicate! 
      private static final NamespacedKey SOULBOUND_KEY = new NamespacedKey("testplugin", "soulbound");

@EventHandler(ignoreCancelled = true)
public void onPlayerDeath(PlayerDeathEvent event) {
    for (Iterator<ItemStack> iterator = event.getDrops().iterator(); iterator.hasNext(); ) {
        ItemStack drop = iterator.next();
        if (drop.getPersistentDataContainer().getOrDefault(SOULBOUND_KEY, PersistentDataType.BOOLEAN, false)) {
            iterator.remove();
            event.getItemsToKeep().add(drop);
        }
    }
}

      Adding an item to this list that the player did not previously have will give them the item on death. An example case could be a "Note" that "You died at X/Y/Z coordinates"

      Returns:
      The list to hold items to keep
